title: Yellow Curry
description: This easy Thai Yellow Curry features tender chicken and vegetables simmered in rich coconut milk with aromatic curry paste. With its perfect balance of sweet and savory flavors, this comforting dish brings authentic Thai cuisine to your dinner table and can easily be adapted for vegan or vegetarian preferences.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon oil (neutral — vegetable, canola, or coconut)
1 pound boneless skinless chicken breasts, cut into bite-sized pieces
2 medium gold potatoes (or about 10 baby gold), cut into 1/2-inch cubes
27 ounces coconut milk (2 × 13.5-ounce cans, full-fat recommended)
1 teaspoon fish sauce (optional; omit for vegan/vegetarian)
Hot cooked rice, for serving (white, jasmine, or brown)
Fresh cilantro and/or sriracha, for garnish
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a large pot or deep skillet over medium heat. Add the 1/2 chopped onion and sauté for 2–3 minutes until softened.
Add the 1 pound chicken pieces and cook, stirring occasionally, for 2–3 minutes until the outsides are no longer pink.
Add the sliced carrot and cubed potatoes and cook for 1–2 minutes more.

Push everything to one side, add the 2 teaspoons grated ginger, 2 cloves minced garlic, and 3–4 tablespoons curry paste to the cleared area, and sauté for 1–2 minutes until fragrant. Stir everything together.
Pour 1 1/2 cans (about 20 ounces) of coconut milk into the pot.
In a small bowl, whisk the 2 teaspoons cornstarch into the remaining 1/2 can (about 7 ounces) of coconut milk until smooth, then add to the pot. Stir to combine.

Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 20–30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through (internal temperature of 165°F / 74°C) and potatoes are fork-tender. If the curry becomes too thick, add water a few tablespoons at a time to thin.

Stir in 1 teaspoon fish sauce, 1 1/2 tablespoons brown sugar, and 1 tablespoon lime juice. Taste and adjust — more sugar for sweetness, more lime for brightness, more fish sauce for depth. Simmer 5 more minutes.
Serve over hot cooked rice. Garnish with fresh cilantro and/or sriracha.
notes: Start with 3 tablespoons curry paste, taste after simmering, and add the 4th if you want more punch. Mae Ploy and Maesri are reliable curry paste brands.
Whisk cornstarch into cold coconut milk before adding to the pot to prevent lumps. Don't add cornstarch directly to the hot pot.
For vegan/vegetarian: Replace chicken with pressed and cubed tofu or chickpeas, omit fish sauce (substitute soy sauce), and add tofu toward the end so it doesn't break apart.
For gluten-free: Use tamari instead of soy sauce if making the vegetarian version, and ensure curry paste is gluten-free.
Also great served over zoodles, spaghetti squash, or quinoa instead of rice.
Wash hands and all surfaces that contact raw chicken. Use a separate cutting board for raw poultry.
